findings.description: The HTML includes fabricated boilerplate metadata not present in the PDF: an HTML <title> and imported stylesheet branding unrelated to the source document.
findings.evidence: HTML head contains "<title>Converted Document</title>" and CSS comment/text "Digital Commons NC — Standalone Theme CSS" plus a Google Fonts import. The PDF pages show only the memorandum content and agency letterhead; no text such as "Converted Document" or "Digital Commons NC" appears in the source.

findings.description: The HTML contains duplicated structural wrapper content with nested <main> elements, creating unnecessary repeated document containers.
findings.evidence: HTML shows "<main> <main> ... </main> </main>". The PDF has a single continuous document structure and no equivalent duplicated container/content wrapper.

findings.description: A sentence in the oral comments summary is broken by injected footer/image markup, disrupting text flow and accuracy of the extracted content.
findings.evidence: PDF reads continuously: "Coal ash should be disposed of on Duke Energy property to avoid additional contamination to other communities." HTML splits this as "Coal ash should be" followed by a page-footer block and image tag, then "disposed of on Duke Energy property..." This interrupts the sentence and degrades textual fidelity.
